title: lepidagathis incana
intro: Lepidagathis incana, a tropical plant species native to South America, is a flowering plant in the family Acanthaceae. Traditionally, Lepidagathis incana has been used for its medicinal properties, which include its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant has been valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing fever and alleviating respiratory issues, and has been used to treat various ailments in traditional medicine. In herbal preparations, the plant's leaves are often made into teas or infusions, while its roots can be dried and used as a decoction for medicinal purposes.
